description: Poisson process theorems from Saporito Ch 3 (poisson_processes).

theorems.name: Poisson Process Marginal Distribution
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.5: For a Poisson process (N_t) with rate λ, N_t ~ Poisson(λt), i.e. P(N_t = j) = e^{-λt} (λt)^j / j!

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.PoissonCounting

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Arrival-time construction of the homogeneous Poisson process: iid
    Exp(rate) interarrival times `ξ` and the counting process `N` they
    generate (`N t = k` iff the k-th arrival has happened by `t` but the
    (k+1)-st has not). The marginal law is NOT a hypothesis. -/
structure PoissonArrivalConstruction {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  prob : IsProbabilityMeasure μ
  rate_pos : 0 < rate
  /-- Interarrival times. -/
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → ℝ
  ξ_meas : ∀ i, Measurable (ξ i)
  ξ_nonneg : ∀ i ω, 0 ≤ ξ i ω
  ξ_law : ∀ i, Measure.map (ξ i) μ = expMeasure rate
  ξ_indep : iIndepFun ξ μ
  /-- The counting process. -/
  N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  count_eq : ∀ t k ω, N t ω = k ↔
    (∑ i ∈ Finset.range k, ξ i ω) ≤ t ∧ t < ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (k + 1), ξ i ω

/-- Theorem 3.3.5 (Poisson marginal law): the one-time marginal of the
    counting process is Poisson(rate·t) — DERIVED from the construction via
    the Erlang law of the arrival times (the Gamma convolution identity) and
    the Gamma-CDF difference identity (`∫₀ᵗ γ_k − ∫₀ᵗ γ_{k+1} =
    e^{−rt}(rt)ᵏ/k!`, an FTC telescope). -/
theorem PoissonArrivalConstruction.marginal_poisson
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (P : PoissonArrivalConstruction μ rate) {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (P.N t) μ = poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t, ht⟩) :=
  haveI := P.prob
  MathFin.PoissonCounting.map_count_eq_poissonMeasure P.rate_pos P.ξ_meas
    P.ξ_nonneg P.ξ_law P.ξ_indep P.count_eq ht

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation via the interarrival construction (the route coverage.md flagged as re-earnable): hypotheses are the textbook construction (iid Exp(rate) interarrivals + the counting relation); the Poisson(rate*t) marginal is DERIVED in MathFin.Foundations.PoissonCounting by composing the Erlang arrival-time law (ErlangSum Gamma-convolution identity) with the new Gamma-CDF difference identity (FTC telescope, absent from Mathlib). Upgraded reduced_core -> full 2026-06-05.

theorems.name: Interarrival Times are iid Exponential
theorems.description: Proposition 3.3.6: For a Poisson process with rate λ, the interarrival times (X_n) are iid Exp(λ)

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.PoissonInterarrival

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Axiomatic homogeneous Poisson counting process carrying its first
    arrival time `τ` (characterised by `τ > t ↔ N t = 0`). -/
structure PoissonProcessWithFirstArrival {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  prob : IsProbabilityMeasure μ
  rate_pos : 0 < rate
  N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (N t)
  zero_at_zero : ∀ ω, N 0 ω = 0
  mono : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → ∀ ω, N s ω ≤ N t ω
  poisson_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N t ω - N s ω) μ
      = poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  independent_increments : ∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
    IndepFun (fun ω => N t ω - N s ω) (fun ω => N v ω - N u ω) μ
  /-- First arrival time. -/
  τ : Ω → ℝ
  τ_meas : Measurable τ
  τ_nonneg : ∀ ω, 0 ≤ τ ω
  τ_first : ∀ ⦃t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ t → {ω | t < τ ω} = {ω | N t ω = 0}

/-- Proposition 3.3.6, first interarrival: `τ ∼ Exp(rate)` — DERIVED from
    the counting axioms (survival law `P(N_t = 0) = e^{−rate·t}` + CDF
    identification). The iid statement for the full interarrival sequence
    needs the strong Markov property (upstream-gated). -/
theorem PoissonProcessWithFirstArrival.first_interarrival_exponential
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (P : PoissonProcessWithFirstArrival μ rate) :
    Measure.map P.τ μ = expMeasure rate :=
  haveI := P.prob
  MathFin.PoissonInterarrival.map_firstArrival_eq_expMeasure P.rate_pos
    P.N_meas P.zero_at_zero P.poisson_increments P.τ_meas P.τ_nonneg P.τ_first

/-- Memorylessness (the exponential signature), DERIVED from independent
    increments: survival of the first arrival factorises over disjoint
    windows. -/
theorem PoissonProcessWithFirstArrival.survival_memoryless
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (P : PoissonProcessWithFirstArrival μ rate)
    {s t : ℝ} (hs : 0 ≤ s) (hst : s ≤ t) :
    μ {ω | P.N t ω = 0}
      = μ {ω | P.N s ω = 0} * μ {ω | P.N t ω - P.N s ω = 0} :=
  MathFin.PoissonInterarrival.survival_factorizes P.zero_at_zero P.mono
    P.independent_increments hs hst

metadata.formalization_scope: Derived core (upgraded 2026-06-05, stays reduced_core honestly): the first interarrival time is PROVED exponential from the counting axioms (MathFin.Foundations.PoissonInterarrival: survival law P(N_t=0)=e^{-rt} read off the increment law, CDF identified against Mathlib cdf_expMeasure_eq), and the memoryless survival factorisation is PROVED from independent increments. What remains assumed-out-of-scope is the textbook claim for the WHOLE interarrival sequence (iid of all ξ_n), which requires the strong Markov property of the process - upstream-gated. Previous version was a pure statement-level spec with the conclusion as a structure field; now the named conclusions are theorems.

theorems.name: Superposition of Poisson Processes
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.9: If (N_i) are independent Poisson processes with rates (λ_i), then N = Σ N_i is a Poisson process with rate Σ λ_i

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.PoissonSuperposition

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Specification of two independent Poisson streams: components N1, N2 have
    Poisson(r1·(t−s)) / Poisson(r2·(t−s)) increments and cross-independence
    between the two streams. The superposition law is NOT a hypothesis: it is
    derived below from the Poisson convolution identity. -/
structure SuperposedPoissonSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  r1 : NNReal
  r2 : NNReal
  rate_decomp : r1 + r2 = rate
  N1 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N2 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N1_zero : ∀ ω, N1 0 ω = 0
  N2_zero : ∀ ω, N2 0 ω = 0
  N1_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (r1 * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  N2_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, (hs0 : 0 ≤ s) → (hst : s ≤ t) →
    Measure.map (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ =
      poissonMeasure (r2 * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩)
  cross_indep : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t →
    IndepFun (fun ω => N1 t ω - N1 s ω) (fun ω => N2 t ω - N2 s ω) μ

/-- Theorem 3.3.9: the superposition of two independent Poisson processes
    with rates r1, r2 is a Poisson process with rate r1 + r2. Derived (not
    assumed) via `poissonMeasure_conv_poissonMeasure`: the sum of independent
    Poisson counts is Poisson at the summed rate. -/
theorem SuperposedPoissonSpec.is_poisson_at_summed_rate
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (S : SuperposedPoissonSpec μ rate)
    {s t : ℝ} (hs : 0 ≤ s) (hst : s ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => (S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) + (S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω)) μ =
      poissonMeasure (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) := by
  have h := MathFin.PoissonSuperposition.indepFun_map_add_poissonMeasure
    (S.cross_indep hs hst) (S.N1_increments hs hst) (S.N2_increments hs hst)
  rwa [← add_mul, S.rate_decomp] at h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation at increment level: the structure encodes only the textbook hypotheses (component increment laws + cross-independence); the superposition conclusion is DERIVED via MathFin.Foundations.PoissonSuperposition (poissonMeasure_conv_poissonMeasure: the Poisson convolution identity, proved from point masses + the binomial theorem, absent from Mathlib; plus the independent-sum-to-convolution bridge). Upgraded reduced_core -> full 2026-06-05.

theorems.name: Splitting (Thinning) of a Poisson Process
theorems.description: Theorem 3.3.10: If a Poisson process with rate λ is split with each event of type i with prob p_i (independently), then the type-i counting process is Poisson(λ p_i) and the streams are independent

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.PoissonThinning

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Specification of a thinned (split) homogeneous Poisson process: the
    process decomposes as N = N1 + N2, and the textbook marking mechanism
    holds per increment — the joint pmf of the two type counts is the
    binomial marking of the Poisson(rate·(t−s)) count
    (`P(ΔN1 = j, ΔN2 = k) = P(ΔN = j+k)·C(j+k,j)pʲ(1−p)ᵏ`). The thinned
    marginal laws and the independence of the streams are NOT hypotheses:
    they are derived below. -/
structure ThinnedPoissonSpec {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (rate : NNReal) where
  /-- Probability of marking each event as type-1. -/
  p : NNReal
  p_le_one : p ≤ 1
  /-- Original Poisson process. -/
  N : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  /-- Type-1 thinned counting process. -/
  N1 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  /-- Type-2 thinned counting process. -/
  N2 : ℝ → Ω → ℕ
  N1_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (N1 t)
  N2_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (N2 t)
  /-- Decomposition: N = N1 + N2 pointwise. -/
  decomposition : ∀ t ω, N t ω = N1 t ω + N2 t ω
  /-- The binomial marking mechanism per increment. -/
  marking : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ s → (hst : s ≤ t) → ∀ j k : ℕ,
    μ {ω | N1 t ω - N1 s ω = j ∧ N2 t ω - N2 s ω = k}
      = ENNReal.ofReal (MathFin.PoissonThinning.markedWeight
          (rate * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) p (j, k))

/-- Theorem 3.3.10: thinning by `p` gives an independent Poisson(p·rate)
    type-1 stream and Poisson((1−p)·rate) type-2 stream. All three
    conclusions are DERIVED from the marking mechanism via the
    binomial-marking factorisation `markedPoissonMeasure_eq_prod`. -/
theorem ThinnedPoissonSpec.thinned_streams_are_independent_poisson
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {rate : NNReal}
    (S : ThinnedPoissonSpec μ rate) {s t : ℝ} (hs : 0 ≤ s) (hst : s ≤ t) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) μ
        = poissonMeasure ((S.p * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) ∧
    Measure.map (fun ω => S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω) μ
        = poissonMeasure (((1 - S.p) * rate) * ⟨t - s, sub_nonneg.mpr hst⟩) ∧
    IndepFun (fun ω => S.N1 t ω - S.N1 s ω) (fun ω => S.N2 t ω - S.N2 s ω) μ := by
  have h := MathFin.PoissonThinning.thinned_streams
    ((S.N1_meas t).sub (S.N1_meas s)) ((S.N2_meas t).sub (S.N2_meas s))
    S.p_le_one (S.marking hs hst)
  rwa [← mul_assoc, ← mul_assoc] at h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation at increment level: the structure encodes only the textbook marking mechanism (joint pmf of the type counts = binomial marking of the Poisson count, the first line of the textbook proof); the thinned marginal laws AND the independence of the streams are DERIVED via MathFin.Foundations.PoissonThinning (markedPoissonMeasure_eq_prod: the binomial-marking factorisation into Poisson(p r) x Poisson((1-p) r), proved pointwise from C(j+k,j)/(j+k)! = 1/(j! k!), absent from Mathlib). Upgraded reduced_core -> full 2026-06-05.

theorems.name: Sum of n Exponentials is Erlang(n, λ)
theorems.description: The n-th arrival time of a Poisson(rate) process — the sum of n iid Exp(rate) interarrival times — has the Erlang(n, rate) = Gamma(n, rate) distribution. Proved from the densities up via the Gamma convolution identity (absent from Mathlib).

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ErlangSum

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

theorem sum_iid_exponentials_is_erlang {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] {r : ℝ} (hr : 0 < r)
    {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) {X : Fin n → Ω → ℝ} (hmeas : ∀ i, Measurable (X i))
    (hlaw : ∀ i, Measure.map (X i) μ = expMeasure r)
    (hindep : iIndepFun X μ) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => ∑ i, X i ω) μ = gammaMeasure n r :=
  MathFin.ErlangSum.sum_iidExp_law_gammaMeasure hr hn hmeas hlaw hindep

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation from primitives in MathFin.Foundations.ErlangSum: the Gamma-exponential convolution identity gammaMeasure_conv_expMeasure (gammaMeasure a r ∗ expMeasure r = gammaMeasure (a+1) r — no Gamma convolution exists in Mathlib; the exponential factors merge so the Beta integral degenerates to ∫x^(a−1) = z^a/a, normalised by Γ(a+1) = a·Γ(a)), then induction over the index finset via IndepFun.map_add_eq_map_conv_map. The previous entry carried the conclusion as a structure field (hypothesis-smuggled); nothing is assumed now. Axioms-clean.
